item.title: Indonesia to quit peace board unless Palestinians benefit
item.description: Indonesian president Prabowo Subianto will quit US president Donald Trump’s Board of Peace if it does not benefit Palestinians, a government statement said, as the country’s involvement faces scrutiny following the US-Israeli war on Iran....

item.title: 4 arrested, DU student detained over attempts to observe March 7
item.description: The police on Saturday arrested four people, including three women, and detained a former Dhaka University student while they were attempting to observe the 55th anniversary of the historic March 7, 1071 speech of the country’s founding president Sheikh Mujibur Rahman....

item.title: BGB, Coast Guard increase patrols to check fuel oil smuggling
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh and the Bangladesh Coast Guard have intensified patrolling and increased check-posts along both land and maritime borders with India and Myanmar to contain fuel oil smuggling as war explodes in the Gulf Arab countries....Geographic Reference

item.title: 10 banks hold 73pc of total defaulted loans
item.description: The top 10 banks in Bangladesh held Tk 4.10 lakh crore in defaulted loans by December 2025, accounting for 73.62 per cent of the country’s total non-performing loans (NPLs), highlighting how the banking crisis has become heavily concentrated in a handful of institutions......

item.title: BB advised to hold rates, reserves amid Mideast crisis
item.description: Top economists have suggested the Bangladesh Bank to prepare for potential economic shocks stemming from the escalating crisis in the Middle East, urging caution on monetary policy and stronger safeguards for foreign exchange reserves......

item.title: 1 killed, 11 hurt during arbitration
item.description: A youth was killed and 11 others were injured in a clash during an arbitration session in Osmani Nagar upazila of Sylhet on Friday night....

item.title: Empowerment of women beyond financial gains stressed
item.description: Women’s empowerment should go beyond financial gains to include building confidence and independence, said Umme Salma Khanam, assistant commissioner and executive magistrate of the Vested Property Cell of Khulna district administration......

item.title: CCCI elections on April 4
item.description: The election to Chattogram Chamber of Commerce and Industry will be held on April 4......

item.title: Farmers rejoice at bumper yield, good market prices
item.description: Due to the bountiful crop yield in the char areas and good market prices of the produce, an Eid atmosphere has been created in the riverside char areas of the Rangpur region, where local residents are now busy buying Eid items......

item.title: India to push ahead with oil imports from Russia
item.description: An Indian government source said Saturday that New Delhi was pushing ahead with imports of Russian oil, after a temporary US waiver to import crude from Moscow due to war in the Middle East......

item.title: Rising US fuel prices risk sparking domestic wildfire for Trump
item.description: Sean Robinson, a 54-year-old schoolteacher in the US capital Washington, did not realise how high gas prices had gotten until he arrived at the pump on Friday......

item.title: Venezuela inflation hits 475pc in 2025
item.description: Venezuelan inflation soared to 475 per cent in 2025, the highest in the world, driven by a tightening of US sanctions in the lead up to the ouster of leader Nicolas Maduro......

item.title: Middle East war a new shock for financial markets
item.description: The outbreak of war in the Middle East has sent shockwaves through financial markets, with energy prices soaring and stocks sliding, just a year after US president Donald Trump’s tariff onslaught rattled investors......

item.title: Int’l Women’s Day today
item.description: Bangladesh observes International Women’s Day today, along with the rest of the world, amid incidents of violence going on against women in the country....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: US hotels seek World Cup boost after tourism dips under Trump
item.description: At the US hotels that Meade Atkeson manages, a drop in tourism weighs heavily on business — but hoteliers like him hope that World Cup enthusiasm will soon eclipse wariness over President Donald Trump’s policies......

item.title: Anthropic vows court fight in Pentagon row
item.description: Anthropic chief executive Dario Amodei has said the company has ‘no choice’ but to challenge in court the Pentagon’s formal designation of the artificial intelligence firm as a risk to US national security......

item.title: Women’s affairs reforms still on paper
item.description: The post-July uprising Bangladesh has witnessed hardly any progress in eliminating discrimination against women and ensuring their rights to property andpoliticalparticipation as the recommendations of the women’s affairs reform commission have......

item.title: Iran war threatens Trump fight with inflation
item.description: US-Israeli strikes in Iran, and Tehran’s retaliation, are set to trigger a surge in US gas prices with a potential knock-on inflationary hit that could pile pressure on president Donald Trump domestically as midterm elections approach......

item.title: Amazon data centre hit highlights Gulf infrastructure vulnerability: experts
item.description: Amazon’s announcement that one of its Gulf data centres had been struck by ‘objects’ in fallout from the US-Israel war against Iran highlights the vulnerability of the critical computing infrastructure, experts told AFP......Election coverage

item.title: Somaliland’s Israel deal could put Berbera port at risk
item.description: Somaliland says its recognition by Israel could be a boon for its Berbera port. But with missiles flying across the region, it could also be a target......

item.title: Chinese consumers scout lower prices, secondhand goods as spending sputters
item.description: In a Shanghai shopping centre, customers browsed racks of used winter coats, $2 trousers and household appliances — pre-used items that would have been out of place in a major Chinese mall a decade ago......

item.title: US sheds jobs in February in warning sign for economy
item.description: The United States unexpectedly lost jobs in February while unemployment edged up, government data showed Friday, piling pressure on president Donald Trump’s economic agenda as key midterm elections approach......

item.title: Myanmar drivers scramble for fuel as Mideast war cuts supply
item.description: Snaking queues of vehicles waited in Myanmar for shrivelling petrol supplies on Wednesday, with some gas stations shuttered as fuel stocks dried up due to the war in the Middle East......

item.title: Prime Bank, Meena IVF partner for healthcare benefits
item.description: Prime Bank PLC recently signed a strategic agreement with Meena IVF and Fertility Care Limited to offer exclusive healthcare benefits to its customers and employees......

item.title: RFL launches 3D-printed gas stove
item.description: Leading business conglomerate RFL launched Bangladesh’s first 3D-printed gas stove at an event on Saturday......

item.title: Islami Bank holds annual risk conference
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC on Saturday organised its annual risk conference at the Islami Bank Tower in the capital......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: EBL, MetLife recognise top bancassurance performers
item.description: Eastern Bank PLC and MetLife Bangladesh recently recognised the top bancassurance performers at Bancassurance Awards Night and Kick Off 2026, celebrating excellence in delivering customer-centric insurance solutions......

item.title: Rape of children, women goes unabated
item.description: Violence against women and children, especially rapes, is continuing unabated across the country in the absence of strict observance of rule of law and due to the existing culture of impunity, according to rights activists....

item.title: Google gives CEO new pay deal worth up to $692m
item.description: The CEO of Google and its parent company Alphabet could earn up to $692 million over the next three years under a new compensation plan published Friday by the US Securities and Exchange Commission......

item.title: Disregard for women’s rights continues: discustion
item.description: Politicians, academics and professionals on Saturday at a seminar in Dhaka said that despite victory was attained in the July uprising in 2024, discriminations against women in society remained as rampant as ever....

item.title: Donating iftar for underprivileged made easier with bKash
item.description: bKash has made it easier for people across the country to contribute to iftar initiatives for underprivileged communities during Ramadan, allowing thousands to donate instantly through its app......Geographic Reference

item.title: Women’s safety and justice
item.description: INTERNATIONAL Women’s Day is a day to challenge the conscience of society. It reminds us that women’s rights, dignity and safety are neither acts of benevolence nor favours. They are fundamental moral demands of human civilisation. The reality is that women have had to struggle for the basic rights for centuries. Through the darkness of discrimination, humiliation......Breaking news alerts

item.title: Cost of waiting
item.description: MARCH 8 is International Women’s Day. And as I sat with that this morning — as a woman, as a humanitarian — I found I couldn’t separate my own reflection from the faces of the women and girls I see every day in the camps of Cox’s Bazar. Perhaps that is exactly as it should be......

item.title: Economic equality for women
item.description: ON THIS crisp spring morning of March 8, 2026, we stand before a world transformed. It is a world of unprecedented technological leaps, echoing with the triumphs of artificial intelligence. Yet, a poignant question remains: in this age of digital supremacy, have we truly attained the pinnacle of human equality?......

item.title: Structural change for women
item.description: ON A busy working day, a woman stands at a crowded bus stop and shifts her scarf slightly, careful to conceal the faint bruise on her arm, the mark she received at home the night before. As she boards the bus, she clutches her bag tighter when the vehicle lurches forward. She is not only trying to keep her balance; she is calculating risk: where to stand, how to......Top news summaries

item.title: Dhaka zoo upgrade plan must first focus animal care
item.description: THE National Zoo in Dhaka has sought Tk 1,260 crore requested for modernisation. The zoo’s director says that the existing infrastructure is outdated and logistical shortage hinder proper animal welfare. The modernisation proposal says that the 75.53-hectare facility, which houses about 3,300 animals and attracts roughly 40–45 lakh visitors each year, would be reorganised......

item.title: Publishers say they focus on content, not authors’ gender
item.description: Publishers on Saturday said that they focus on the content and skills of writings, instead of writers’ gender....

item.title: Trust the body that saved you
item.description: ‘WHY didn’t you just move his hand away?’ my friend asks. I stare, my mind blank. I want to answer, but I can’t. Seriously, why didn’t I just move it? Or shout? As I search for the reason, a lump rises in my throat. I hear the care in my friend’s voice, but my mind drifts to the moment I froze. I am reliving the violation, the humiliation!......Breaking news alerts

item.title: When transition becomes a gendered battlefield
item.description: POLITICAL transitions are often framed as moments of democratic possibility, new rules, new actors, new beginnings. In Bangladesh, as in many post-authoritarian or transitional contexts, the language of reform, neutrality, and renewal has dominated public discourse. Yet beneath this rhetoric lies a familiar contradiction: women are highly visible inpoliticalconversations......

item.title: Fear of the ‘bitch’
item.description: THROUGHOUT the July uprising and the authoritarian Awami League regime, women acted not as mere representatives but as agents of rupture; yet political parties and the practices of statecraft denied them substantive presence in the new parliament. Women’s active participation in the resistance was central — they organised, documented, and sustained political life......

item.title: Why BNP is not our feminist alternative
item.description: I KEEP thinking about the conversations with my progressive and feminist friends and allies right before the 2026 election results in Bangladesh. Many were holding their breath because of the prospect of a Jamaat-e-Islami-led government. The Jamaat-e-Islami ameer Shafiqur Rahman’s pre-election remarks about restricting women to work for five hours instead of eight......Top news summaries

item.title: Who gets to author Bangladesh’s transition?
item.description: TODAY, as Bangladesh joins the world in observing International Women’s Day, this Women’s Day arrives in the shadow of July 2024 — a political rupture whose meaning, authorship, and outcomes are still being negotiated. Political transitions are often commemorated as moments of progress, yet they also expose how fragile and contingent women’s gains remain......

item.title: Woman found dead on Lauhajang bank in Tangail
item.description: The police have recovered the body of a woman named Samna Begum, 55, from the banks of the Lauhajang River in Tangail city....Breaking news alerts

item.title: 2 children go missing in Kushtia river
item.description: TWO children went missing while bathing in the Gorai River near the Khoksa-Osmanpur ferry ghat in Kushtia on Saturday afternoon....

item.title: Barishal hospital employee suspended for slapping minor patient in OT
item.description: Authorities at the Sher-e-Bangla Medical College Hospital in Barishal suspended a staff member on Saturday after he allegedly slapped a child patient inside the operation theatre during treatment....

item.title: Govt committed to solving expatriates’ problems: Arif
item.description: Expatriates’ welfare and overseas employment minister Ariful Haque Chowdhury on Saturday said that the government is committed to solving the problems of expatriates that emerged due to cancellation of flights....

item.title: Chuadanga Life Care Hospital sealed, fined Tk 2 lakh
item.description: A mobile court on Saturday raided a private hospital at Damurhuda upazila in Chuadanga and sealed its operation theatre, imposing Tk 2 lakh fine over serious irregularities and lack of proper documentation....Daily news updates

item.title: 2 held with firearm in Noakhali
item.description: The police in a drive have arrested two people with a foreign-made pistol and two rounds of live ammunition at Kanchihata under Begumganj in Noakhali....

item.title: Coast Guard nabs nine, seizes Tk 3cr hemp
item.description: Bangladesh Coast Guard members arrested nine drug smugglers along with 10 kg and 400 grams of hemp worth about Tk 3 lakh and 12 thousand....

item.title: Stepfather killed Narsingdi girl, not rapists: police
item.description: The stepfather of a teenage girl, whose body was found in a mustard field in Narsingdi’s Madhabdi, has confessed in court to murdering her to avoid further social humiliation following a rape incident, according to the police....

item.title: Strong primary health care, governance stressed
item.description: Public health experts and health rights activists on Saturday stressed the need for a strong primary healthcare network and greater accountability to bring Bangladesh under Universal Health Coverage....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Russian strikes kill 12 across Ukraine
item.description: Russia fired a volley of missiles and drones across Ukraine overnight on Friday to Saturday, killing 12 people and wounding more than a dozen, including children....

item.title: NASA test changes asteroid orbit
item.description: Four years ago, NASA purposely smashed a spacecraft into a small asteroid to see if they could deflect it—a test to prove humanity could protect Earth from threatening space rocks....

item.title: India to support inclusive Bangladesh: Verma
item.description: The high commissioner of India to Bangladesh, Pranay Verma, on Saturday said that India had always supported and would continue to support a democratic, stable, progressive and inclusive Bangladesh....

item.title: 7 Indians among 11 held while entering India illegally
item.description: Members of the Border Guard Bangladesh have detained 11 people, including seven Indian nationals, while they were attempting to enter India illegally through the Maheshpur border in Jhenaidah....

item.title: Ex-adviser Mahfuj launches new political platform ‘Alternatives’
item.description: A newpoliticalplatform, Alternatives, led by Mahfuj Alam, former information adviser to the interim government, was launched on Friday....Politics

item.title: 28 more Middle East flights cancelled
item.description: At least 22 more Middle Eastern flights were cancelled to and from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka city on Saturday, raising the total number of flights cancelled to 296 in the past eight days since February 28....

item.title: NU extends professional master’s application deadline
item.description: The National University has extended the deadline for submitting online preliminary applications for admission to professional master’s programmes at colleges affiliated with the NU for the 2026 academic year until 12:00 midnight on March 16....Breaking news alerts

item.title: British Council’s WoW grant recipients’ names announced
item.description: The British Council has announced the awarding of 13 grants as part of Women of the World Bangladesh 2025–26....

item.title: 2 JCD leaders arrested over extortion, assault of college teacher
item.description: The police on Friday night arrested two leaders of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, the student wing of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, on charges of assaulting a college teacher after failing to extort money from him....

item.title: Two minor girls rescued, 2 still missing in Kushtia’s Gorai River
item.description: Two of the four children who went missing while bathing in the Gorai River at Khoksa in Kushtia were rescued alive on Saturday, while efforts are under way to locate the two others still missing....

item.title: Falling egg prices force shutdown of small poultry farms in Rajshahi
item.description: Small-scale poultry farmers in Rajshahi and neighbouring districts are incurring persistent loss as egg prices have dropped significantly below production costs, forcing many grassroots producers to shut down their farms....Daily news updates

item.title: IU TEACHER MURDER: Two teachers, one official suspended
item.description: The authorities of Islamic University in Kushtia on Saturday suspended two teachers and a university official after their names surfaced in the case of killing the university’s Social Welfare department chairperson Asma Sadia Runa....

item.title: Hasna Hossain Akhi receives GSWL award
item.description: Dr Hasna Hossain Akhi received the Global Star Women Leadership award in the medical care category in recognition of her contribution to women’s health, especially in the treatment of PCOS and infertility, at a ceremony at the Bishwa Sahitya Kendra auditorium in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: Thalassaemia spreads as prevention plan stalls
item.description: Thalassaemia, a genetic blood disorder that is considered relatively rare worldwide, is gradually spreading in the country because of limited screening, weak prevention measures and costly treatment....

item.title: Khaleda Zia to be conferred best indomitable woman in advancing democracy
item.description: The Ministry of Women and Children Affairs will confer the Best Indomitable Woman in Advancing Democracy award on the late prime minister Khaleda Zia....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Zakat can be tool for targeted poverty alleviation, says PM
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Saturday emphasised the potential of the zakat system in reducing poverty, saying that Bangladesh could serve as a model for the Islamic world in this regard....
